default.description: Core-plugin permissions shared by both webviews: `listen` for backend events plus the rest of `core:default`. Everything else (tray, global shortcut, autostart, notifications, window show/hide, update probes) is driven from Rust and needs no JS capability. The app's own `invoke` commands are NOT granted here — they are scoped per window in `palette.json` (main) and `settings.json` (settings) so a compromise of one webview cannot reach the other's command surface.

palette-commands.description: App commands the palette (`main`) webview is allowed to invoke: search, the paste/copy/delete/pin row actions, preview hydration, the AI action inspector, the status-bar capture toggle and Settings launcher, and the shared read-only stores (settings / permissions / capabilities / last hotkey failure). It deliberately omits every privileged settings/installer command (`update_settings`, `install_cli`, `request_accessibility`, the updater and semantic-index controls) so a compromised palette webview cannot reach them.

settings-commands.description: App commands the settings (`settings`) webview is allowed to invoke: persisting settings, the password-manager preset, the AI availability / semantic-index controls, the Accessibility prompt, the updater check, the CLI installer, and the shared read-only stores (settings / permissions / capabilities / last hotkey failure). It deliberately omits every palette action command (search, paste/copy/delete/pin, preview, the AI action inspector) so a compromised settings webview cannot drive clipboard side effects.
